summary: Confirmation Email Idempotence Fix - All 14 tests passed. The critical bug fix ensures confirmation emails are now sent from BOTH the webhook path AND the polling fallback path. The send_confirmation_email_once() helper uses MongoDB atomic update with $ne True for exactly-once delivery. Video emails have 'Confirmer ma presence et rejoindre' button with proof_link. Physical emails have 'Je suis arrive' CTA button.

backend: Idempotence: calling twice sends email only once (confirmation_email_sent flag)
MongoDB atomic update with $ne True prevents race condition duplicates
Video email: proof_link present, 'Confirmer ma presence et rejoindre' button present, ICS button present
Physical email: 'Je suis arrive' button present, NO proof_link, ICS button present
GET /api/invitations/{token}/guarantee-status returns correct status and is_guaranteed fields
Webhook path imports and calls send_confirmation_email_once
Helper returns False when confirmation_email_sent is already True
Helper rolls back flag on email failure so other path can retry

seed_data_creation: Created temporary test participants and appointments for idempotence testing (cleaned up after tests)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Confirmation email idempotence fix verified. The send_confirmation_email_once() helper at line 26-100 in invitations.py is called from: (1) webhook path in webhooks.py line 121, (2) polling path in check_guarantee_status line 467, (3) direct accept path in respond_to_invitation line 414. The helper uses MongoDB atomic update with $ne True condition on confirmation_email_sent field for concurrency safety. On email failure, the flag is rolled back ($unset) so the other path can retry. Video emails have proof_link and 'Confirmer ma presence et rejoindre' button. Physical emails have 'Je suis arrive' CTA button with invitation_link for check-in.
